feed.title: Herb Bowie's Blog
feed.subtitle: From the Desk of H. Bowie

entry.title: Released Notenik Version 18.9.0
entry.content:
Added a new Image Layout field; Fixed a Folder exclusion bug; Documented a new Use Case for Presenting with Notenik; Added a new Nav Slug that can be used to access navigational info within a Merge Template; Fixed optional YAML representation of Wiki Links and Backlinks; Provided a new option to choose a desired text editor from a curated list.
related link: https://notenik.app/kb/version-18.9.0.html
#Notenik #Pkm

entry.title: Released Notenik Version 18.8.0
entry.content:
Improved Notenik app icon to better support dark, clear and tinted styles; default Tahoe menu item images have been restored; added support for smart quotes in titles; additional slide styling for presentation display mode; added a toggle to actually present a presentation; made a few default CSS styling improvements.
related link: https://notenik.app/kb/version-18.8.0.html
#Notenik #Pkm

entry.title: Released Notenik Version 18.7.0
entry.content:
Massaged the title parser a bit more to respect embedded HTML line breaks, but mostly this release gets Notenik a little bit closer to being useful for preparing and delivering a presentation (aka "slide deck").
related link: https://notenik.app/kb/version-18.7.0.html
#Notenik #Pkm

entry.title: Published "On The Passing of Paul Ehrlich"
entry.content:
For some he seemed to be a hero, while others considered him to be a demon. Let's dive below the surface to see if we can unearth a more nuanced view.
related link: https://hbowie.net/writings/on-the-passing-of-paul-ehrlich.html
#Population

entry.title: Released Notenik Version 18.6.0
entry.content:
Added an optional menu bar extra (for the right side of the menu bar); fixed limited Markdown parsing of the Title field to leave underscores within words undisturbed.
related link: https://notenik.app/kb/version-18.6.0.html
#Notenik #Pkm

entry.title: Published "Why AI is Different"
entry.content:
Eight ways in which AI is different from prior tech advances. My most surprising conclusion: "AI is effectively a societal regression from information agriculture to information foraging."
related link: https://hbowie.net/writings/why-ai-is-different.html
#Ai #Tech

entry.title: Released Notenik Version 18.5.0
entry.content:
Fixed an image-related bug in the Web Book Export as Site function; Eliminated unnecessary image icons in menu items; Added option for resolving relative wiki links when using the Share with Options function; Refinement of styling handling in note titles; Refinement of file naming to accommodate some meaningful punctuation characters.
related link: https://notenik.app/kb/version-18.5.0.html
#Notenik #Pkm

entry.title: Published The Value of Autonomy
entry.content:
I think we all understand instinctively why some degree of autonomy is important to every human: freedom feels good, and constraints often feel stifling and restrictive. But why is autonomy important *to our species*? Why, in other words, did we humans evolve to value autonomy, and to so regularly exercise it?
related link: https://abouthumans.net/the-value-of-autonomy.html
#Humanism

entry.title: Published An Appreciation for The Thin Man
entry.content:
In my judgment, this is one of those rare cases where the film is actually better than the book, without sacrificing much of value from the written text. I’ve watched the film repeatedly over the years and my appreciation for it has only grown with each viewing.
related link: https://hbowie.net/writings/an-appreciation-for-the-thin-man.html
#Film

entry.title: Finished reading *Humanly Possible: Seven Hundred Years of Humanist Freethinking, Inquiry, and Hope* by Sarah Bakewell
entry.content:
An informative, enlightening and interesting account of the history of humanistic thought, introducing us to many of the significant figures originally giving voice to such ideas.
rating: ★★★★★
related link: https://literatibookstore.com/book/9780735223394
#Books #Humanism

entry.title: Published "My Education in Racial Superiority"
entry.content:
I am, to all appearances, white, and my ancestry, as far as it can be determined, is mostly European.
But please don’t hold that against me.
related link: https://hbowie.net/writings/my-education-in-racial-superiority.html
#Humanism #Race

entry.title: Released Notenik Version 18.4.0
entry.content:
Provided a way to use subfolders, but specify some that should not be treated as Notenik collections (in response to a user request). Plus some other minor refinements.
related link: https://notenik.app/kb/version-18.4.0.html
#Notenik #Pkm

entry.title: Published "Over Seventy Important Things"
entry.content:
My new web book, at AboutHumans.net, now catalogs over seventy Important Things to Know About Humans! This is a crazy number, because we usually oversimplify our understandings of our own natures to fit into some straightforward models. But we are complex beasts!
related link: https://abouthumans.net/over-seventy-important-things.html
#Humanism

entry.title: Released Notenik Version 18.3.0
entry.content:
Various and sundry improvements including some new/enhanced provisions for outlining. Also a new starter pack focused on use of the PARA organizational method (making good use of outlining).
related link: https://notenik.app/kb/version-18.3.0.html
#Notenik #Pkm

entry.title: Published "New Speedway Boogie"
entry.content:
"New Speedway Boogie" is a song prompted by the tragedy at Altamont over fifty years ago, but it has plenty of meaning for us today as well.
related link: https://hbowie.net/writings/new-speedway-boogie.html
#GratefulDead #Songs

entry.title: Released Notenik Version 18.2.0
entry.content:
Added new Markdown commands to generate useful HTML head metadata when exporting a Web Book as a site.
related link: https://notenik.app/kb/version-18.2.0.html
#Notenik #Pkm

entry.title: Released Notenik Version 18.1.0
entry.content:
Additional improvements targeted on web book publishing.
related link: https://notenik.app/kb/version-18.1.0.html
#Notenik #Pkm

entry.title: Finished reading *The Hunter* by Tana French
entry.content:
This is a sequel to *The Searcher*, featuring the same characters. Pretty good, but not quite as fun as the first book, since we already know the main characters, and they don't evolve a whole lot in this book.
rating: ★★★
related link: https://www.theguardian.com/books/2024/mar/11/the-hunter-by-tana-french-review-sequel-to-the-searcher
#Books

entry.title: Released Notenik Version 18.0.0
entry.content:
Further improvements mostly focused on web book publishing.
related link: https://notenik.app/kb/version-18.0.0.html
#Notenik #Pkm

entry.title: Released Notenik Version 17.9.0
entry.content:
Some additional Tahoe refinements, plus some improvements for web book publishing.
related link: https://notenik.app/kb/version-17.9.0.html
#Notenik #Pkm
